page: Microsoft Azure D5_v2 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Dv2 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The CPU operates at a base frequency of 2.3 GHz and is equipped with a 50 MB L3 cache. It supports hardware-assisted technologies such as Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX), AES cryptography, and Intel Advanced Vector Extensions, but lacks AVX-512 capabilities.

The instance is configured with 56.0 GB of system memory, providing a resource density of 3.5 GB of RAM per core. For local data requirements, the server includes 819 GB of local storage. No graphics processing units (GPUs) are bundled with this instance type, making it reliant entirely on CPU-based execution.

Performance benchmarks indicate a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core execution. Single-core CPU performance, measured via stress-ng and Geekbench, falls into the lower 25th percentile. However, multi-core benchmarks, including PassMark CPU and Geekbench multi-core tests, reach average mid-tier performance levels. Memory read bandwidth is average at 58.0 GB/sec for large blocks, though write bandwidth for large blocks is weaker, placing in the bottom 25%. Database operations and static web serving throughput perform in the middle 50% tier, with Redis SET operations achieving 18.19 million operations per second.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D5_v2 offers a legacy hardware profile that trades raw single-threaded speed for a high volume of dedicated physical cores and substantial local storage capacity. This makes it a viable option for multi-threaded workloads that do not require modern instruction sets like AVX-512. Recommended workloads include general-purpose web serving, multi-threaded file decompression, database operations, and legacy enterprise applications that benefit from dedicated core allocations and local storage.
description: Microsoft Azure D5_v2 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the Dv2 family, powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 processor with 16 dedicated physical cores on the x86_64 architecture. Operating at 2.3 GHz with a 50 MB L3 cache, this instance is equipped with 56.0 GB of RAM and 819 GB of local storage, with no GPU acceleration. Benchmark data reveals weak single-core CPU performance, but average multi-core capabilities, memory read bandwidth, and database operations. The server achieves 18.19 million operations per second in Redis SET tests and 14.84 GB/sec in static web serving. Given its legacy hardware profile, the D5_v2 provides a cost-effective option for multi-threaded workloads that benefit from dedicated physical cores and local storage without requiring high single-threaded speeds. It is best suited for general-purpose web serving, database operations, and multi-threaded enterprise applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D5_v2 is a Dv2-family virtual machine featuring 16 dedicated Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 cores, 56 GB of RAM, and 819 GB of local storage, offering balanced multi-core performance for general-purpose workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D5_v2 is a general-purpose VM with 16 Intel Xeon cores, 56 GB RAM, and 819 GB local storage, optimized for multi-threaded workloads.
tagline: A dedicated sixteen-core legacy instance offering balanced memory, substantial local storage, and reliable multi-threaded performance for general-purpose enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 physical cores at 2.3 GHz.
56 GB of system memory and 819 GB of local storage.
Average multi-core CPU and memory read bandwidth performance.
Weak single-core performance and large-block memory write speeds.
Well-suited for database operations, static web serving, and legacy applications.
